title: Fly Fishing from a Boat on Lake Puelo in Chubut Under Lush Forest Canopy
description: Lake Puelo, Chubut: Two individuals navigate the turquoise, glacial waters in a vibrant blue and white skiff named Tucu Tucu while engaging in the rhythmic tradition of fly fishing. The foreground is dominated by the gentle rippling of the water, which reflects the verdant, densely packed foliage of the overhanging Patagonian forest that lines the steep shoreline. Samuel Jeffery stands near the bow, focused intently on casting a delicate line toward the shadows cast by the dense canopy, his posture reflecting the practiced patience required for this Andean sport. Audrey Bergner sits toward the stern, expertly maneuvering the boat to ensure their silent drift maintains a strategic distance from the shoreline's intricate branch structures. The lighting, soft and diffused by the surrounding mountain geography, highlights the intense color of the water and the rich, mossy textures of the temperate rainforest. Such scenes illustrate the intersection of local recreational heritage and the pristine, protected wilderness areas that define the aesthetic and ecological character of Argentina's southern lake district. The quiet concentration shared between the pair emphasizes the meditative quality of outdoor life in this remote frontier, where the silence is broken only by the hum of the water and the soft flick of the line. This image captures the authentic spirit of Patagonian exploration, highlighting the deep connection between travelers and the complex, living landscapes they traverse. This image is a collaborative production by Samuel Jeffery and Audrey Bergner for Project 23.
